subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: triangulated across multilateral bodies (IMF Article IV 2025, FATF, World Justice Project, Transparency International, Freedom House, RSF) + Panama official sources (INEC, Contraloría, Superintendencia de Bancos, ACP) + Panamanian/regional independent press (La Prensa, La Estrella, Al Jazeera, NACLA).

Source-lean: the canal-sovereignty and port-deal coverage is heavily US/UK-framed; counterweighted with Panamanian and regional outlets, but the geopolitical scores carry residual US-press lean (confidence held moderate there).
Dollarization is the defining macro fact: no monetary autonomy, but no devaluation risk and low inflation.

economic.notes: Dollarized stability and a large banking/logistics hub, against a fiscal hit from the 2024 copper-mine closure and structural canal-water risk.

fiscal_state.notes: The 2024 deficit hit ~7.4% GDP (from 3.9%), driven by the Cobre Panamá mine closure; debt ~50-55% GDP. New fiscal-responsibility ceilings (4% in 2025 to 1.5% by 2030) are credible if enforced. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Dollarized: no central bank, imports US Fed policy wholesale, zero countercyclical tool - scored negative for lost autonomy (the offsetting no-devaluation-risk benefit is captured under inflation and sanctions). (IMF Art IV)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Not an issuer, but full USD usage confers hard-currency stability and no FX/convertibility risk - a mild positive. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Near-zero/negative CPI (the dollarization anchor): -0.2% YoY end-2024, -0.7% by mid-2025. Low and durable. (IMF Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: A large international banking center (~$160bn assets, 60+ international banks, liquidity well above minimum); removed from the FATF grey list (Oct 2023) and EU high-risk list; the lack of a lender of last resort is the structural caveat. (Superintendencia de Bancos, FATF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: The Canal recovered to full draft (FY25 revenue ~$5.7bn, +14%) and the Colón Free Zone is a major re-export hub; offset by structural canal-watershed water risk and the closed copper mine (resource upside gone). (ACP; Colón Free Zone)

institutional.notes: Entrenched corruption and weak courts, partly offset by a genuinely insulated, world-class Canal Authority and a press that remains plural.

rule_of_law.notes: Ranks poorly on rule of law (low among high-income peers on WJP); entrenched top-level corruption and impunity, weak commercial courts. (WJP, Freedom House 2025, TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: INEC and the Contraloría are broadly credible and used by the IMF; the Contraloría has shown institutional teeth. (Contraloría; IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A genuinely world-class, insulated Canal Authority, but politicized line ministries and weak judicial administration drag the average. (Freedom House; US State ICS)

press_freedom.notes: Generally free, plural press (La Prensa, La Estrella), but defamation laws and a 2025 protest-era rights suspension in Bocas del Toro are warning signs. (RSF; Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: A legitimately elected but contested government amid 2025 pension-reform unrest, with a favorable residency pathway and a sharply de-escalated migration crisis.

political_stability.notes: Mulino (since July 2024) governs but with very low approval; a 2025 pension-reform law triggered 70+ days of strikes and a temporary constitutional-rights suspension in Bocas del Toro; legitimate but contested. (Al Jazeera; NACLA)

civil_liberties.notes: Generally respected, but 2025 protest crackdowns, union repression, and a rights suspension are real erosion. (Freedom House; OHCHR)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: The Friendly Nations Visa (which includes US citizens) is a lawful, durable pathway; a 2021 reform raised the bar (two-year provisional to permanent; property/deposit or job) and 2025 trimmed the temporary permit to six months - accessible, if less frictionless than before. (Embassy of Panama; immigration firms)

social_violence.notes: Homicide ~13/100k (concentrated in Panamá/Colón), moderate by regional standards and slowly rising; the Darién Gap migration crisis collapsed (~-99% in 2025), a major de-risking. (INEC; InSight Crime; DHS)

demographics.notes: Median age ~30, ~70% urban, population growing ~1.5%/yr; a favorable working-age dividend. (UNFPA)

geopolitical.notes: Open capital account and no external war, but uniquely exposed as the object of great-power contestation over the canal and its ports.

alliance_reliability.notes: The US is the essential partner, but the current US administration's canal-sovereignty rhetoric, China-influence claims, and pressure forcing the CK Hutchison port divestment make the relationship coercive rather than reliable. (Atlantic Council; CFR; FT)

conflict_involvement.notes: No external war and no military, but uniquely exposed as the object of US-China contestation over the canal and ports. (CFR)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Fully open capital account, dollarized, no controls; post-FATF clean. (IMF; FATF)

physical_practical.notes: Hurricane-free with a strong capital-city core, against a material and recurring canal-watershed drought risk and thin rural services.

climate.notes: Canal drought (2023-24 draft restrictions) plus dual-coast plus watershed stress, relatively higher readiness. (WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Strong private care in Panama City (a regional medical hub), uneven and thin in rural/indigenous areas. (Freedom House; US State ICS)

infrastructure.notes: Panama City is excellent (metro, airport hub, telecom, logistics); rural areas lag. (logistics/trade sources)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate; safe expat enclaves but specific high-crime zones in Panama City/Colón and rising petty crime. (OSAC; INEC)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low seismic and no hurricanes reduce catastrophe exposure; drought/flood risk and an underbuilt insurance market roughly offset. (reinsurance context)

summary: Dollarized stability (low inflation, no devaluation or convertibility risk, an open capital account), a large international banking and logistics hub, a favorable Friendly Nations residency pathway, and favorable demographics. The drags are weak rule of law and entrenched corruption, a fiscal hole opened by the 2024 copper-mine closure, contested politics after 2025 pension-reform unrest, a structural canal-watershed drought, and a US relationship turned coercive over canal and port sovereignty.
